title: buildah-copy does not properly follow canonical symbolic links
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

buildah copy does not properly follow canonicalized symbolic links (i.e. linksrc -> /opt/linktarget).  This is in contrast to docker and its COPY statement, which does operate correctly.



<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. run the buildah script
```
#!/bin/bash

set -o errexit

container=$(buildah from alpine:latest)
mountpoint=$(buildah mount $container)

buildah run "${container}" -- mkdir -p /opt/testcase/target
buildah run "${container}" -- ln -s target /opt/testcase/link-relative
buildah run "${container}" -- ln -s /opt/testcase/target /opt/testcase/link-canonical

buildah run "${container}" -- cat /etc/hosts
buildah run "${container}" -- ls -al /opt/testcase

buildah copy --chown nobody:nobody "${container}" /bin/false /opt/testcase/target/test0-baseline
buildah copy --chown nobody:nobody "${container}" /bin/false /opt/testcase/link-relative/test1-relative
buildah copy --chown nobody:nobody "${container}" /bin/false /opt/testcase/link-canonical/test2-canonical

buildah run "${container}" -- find /opt/testcase -exec ls -ald {} \;


#buildah commit --format oci "$container" buildah-containerdetection
buildah unmount "$container"
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Buildah failed when trying to evaluate the canonical link: 
`error adding content to container "alpine-working-container": error creating directory "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/61ad97f257af535468c4f2d7740e15155845b7c0aa95efe46fa9c2b0bba61a3f/merged/opt/testcase/link-canonical": chown /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/61ad97f257af535468c4f2d7740e15155845b7c0aa95efe46fa9c2b0bba61a3f/merged/opt/testcase/link-canonical: no such file or directory`

**Describe the results you expected:**
The buildah script should properly evaluate the canonical link in the context of the container's rootfs, and correctly copy the file to the target destination.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.9.0-1.el7.centos.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.9.0
Go Version:      go1.10.3
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
Git Commit:      
Built:           Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
not a podman build issue
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
# cat /etc/centos-release
CentOS Linux release 7.6.1810 (Core) 
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux ada.ca.alcatel-lucent.com 3.10.0-957.27.2.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Jul 29 17:46:05 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information
# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# Storage options to be passed to underlying storage drivers

# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"

# Remap-UIDs/GIDs is the mapping from UIDs/GIDs as they should appear inside of
# a container, to UIDs/GIDs as they should appear outside of the container, and
# the length of the range of UIDs/GIDs.  Additional mapped sets can be listed
# and will be heeded by libraries, but there are limits to the number of
# mappings which the kernel will allow when you later attempt to run a
# container.
#
# remap-uids = 0:1668442479:65536
# remap-gids = 0:1668442479:65536

# Remap-User/Group is a name which can be used to look up one or more UID/GID
# ranges in the /etc/subuid or /etc/subgid file.  Mappings are set up starting
# with an in-container ID of 0 and the a host-level ID taken from the lowest
# range that matches the specified name, and using the length of that range.
# Additional ranges are then assigned, using the ranges which specify the
# lowest host-level IDs first, to the lowest not-yet-mapped container-level ID,
# until all of the entries have been used for maps.
#
# remap-user = "storage"
# remap-group = "storage"

[storage.options.thinpool]
# Storage Options for thinpool

# autoextend_percent determines the amount by which pool needs to be
# grown. This is specified in terms of % of pool size. So a value of 20 means
# that when threshold is hit, pool will be grown by 20% of existing
# pool size.
# autoextend_percent = "20"

# autoextend_threshold determines the pool extension threshold in terms
# of percentage of pool size. For example, if threshold is 60, that means when
# pool is 60% full, threshold has been hit.
# autoextend_threshold = "80"

# basesize specifies the size to use when creating the base device, which
# limits the size of images and containers.
# basesize = "10G"

# blocksize specifies a custom blocksize to use for the thin pool.
# blocksize="64k"

# directlvm_device specifies a custom block storage device to use for the
# thin pool. Required if you setup devicemapper
# directlvm_device = ""

# directlvm_device_force wipes device even if device already has a filesystem
# directlvm_device_force = "True"

# fs specifies the filesystem type to use for the base device.
# fs="xfs"

# log_level sets the log level of devicemapper.
# 0: LogLevelSuppress 0 (Default)
# 2: LogLevelFatal
# 3: LogLevelErr
# 4: LogLevelWarn
# 5: LogLevelNotice
# 6: LogLevelInfo
# 7: LogLevelDebug
# log_level = "7"

# min_free_space specifies the min free space percent in a thin pool require for
# new device creation to succeed. Valid values are from 0% - 99%.
# Value 0% disables
# min_free_space = "10%"

# mkfsarg specifies extra mkfs arguments to be used when creating the base
# device.
# mkfsarg = ""

# mountopt specifies extra mount options used when mounting the thin devices.
# mountopt = ""

# use_deferred_removal Marking device for deferred removal
# use_deferred_removal = "True"

# use_deferred_deletion Marking device for deferred deletion
# use_deferred_deletion = "True"

# xfs_nospace_max_retries specifies the maximum number of retries XFS should
# attempt to complete IO when ENOSPC (no space) error is returned by
# underlying storage device.
# xfs_nospace_max_retries = "0"
```

comments.body: @kedmison thanks for the issue report.  @jhonce is this similar to your recent Podmand glob copy change?

from the buildah script in the issue description, before running `buildah copy --chown nobody:nobody "${container}" /bin/false /opt/testcase/link-canonical/test2-canonical`, I run ls and see the link `/opt/testcase/target` is red and flashing, does this mean this link is problematic, should copy work under this situation?

```
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root   root     20 Sep 16 14:50 link-canonical -> /opt/testcase/target
```

It means the link cannot be resolved, but that is exactly the problem.  when run from chroot $mountpoint, it should work (as the canonical link would resolve to the right location).  But, it appears that buildah copy does not properly follow canonical symbolic links on the target filesystem, so the link appears broken and is shown in red.

If you take the mkdir, ln, and ls commands out of the buildah script and run them on any host, they will work and show the desired behavior.

failed to reproduce on master, this issue should be fixed on master branch.

```
$ buildah unshare
# ./canonical.bash
# Generated by Buildah
127.0.0.1   localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1         localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
total 12
drwxr-xr-x    3 root     root          4096 Sep 24 13:19 .
drwxr-xr-x    3 root     root          4096 Sep 24 13:19 ..
lrwxrwxrwx    1 root     root            20 Sep 24 13:19 link-canonical -> /opt/testcase/target
lrwxrwxrwx    1 root     root             6 Sep 24 13:19 link-relative -> target
drwxr-xr-x    2 root     root          4096 Sep 24 13:19 target
8f6d8c7fcc05e6ab67718dce2a4e32d423fd5d311283c777d396b6092a6bf027
9306c447372ed163bdb5019a62ba04211871c297db8b136844fbe5f9ecc7c6ac
88fdb6dee4f6271b8e69f4319aa72c06e6766782652ab6f2a94e095d0add70d9
drwxr-xr-x    3 root     root          4096 Sep 24 13:19 /opt/testcase
lrwxrwxrwx    1 root     root            20 Sep 24 13:19 /opt/testcase/link-canonical -> /opt/testcase/target
drwxr-xr-x    2 root     root          4096 Sep 24 13:19 /opt/testcase/target
-rwxr-xr-x    1 nobody   nobody       73840 May  3 14:41 /opt/testcase/target/test2-canonical
-rwxr-xr-x    1 nobody   nobody       73840 May  3 14:41 /opt/testcase/target/test1-relative
-rwxr-xr-x    1 nobody   nobody       73840 May  3 14:41 /opt/testcase/target/test0-baseline
lrwxrwxrwx    1 root     root             6 Sep 24 13:19 /opt/testcase/link-relative -> target
# buildah version
Version:         1.12.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.12.7
Image Spec:      1.0.1
Runtime Spec:    1.0.1-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.1
Git Commit:      c3b1ec6b
Built:           Tue Sep 24 09:17:43 2019
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

This was 100% reproducible in my test environment, so I'm eager to test the master build.  
Thanks for the excellent instructions for building master; I'll try building it and confirming it's fixed there.

@kedmison yes. canonial.bash was the script you supplied
as non-root user, I got error ```cannot mount using driver overlay in rootless mode. You need to run it in a `buildah unshare` session```, so I ran `buildah unshare`. If I'm root I can directly run the script.
